title: How to summarize recovery work after a setup incident (recommended approach)
summary: Summarize recovery by leading with the most confusing user state (partial installs) and giving one safe next action, while clearly segmenting by environment (macOS/Linux verified; Windows still validating). Avoid claiming universal resolution until Windows recovery is confirmed, and ensure communications point to the patched CLI recovery flow—not the old installer path.

claims.text: Lead with symptoms (especially partial installs) because they were the biggest source of confusion, then provide one safe next action users can take without risking state/credential corruption.

Do not describe the incident as universally “resolved” unless you prominently caveat that recovery has only been verified on macOS and Linux; Windows recovery steps are still being validated due to an unresolved credential-cache issue.

Structure the message into: who is affected, what action to take, and which environments are pending validation; place the Windows caveat in the first paragraph (not a footnote) to prevent customers interpreting/forwarding it as proof of universal recovery.

Promote the patched CLI’s recovery command as the primary recovery mechanism because it checks stale credentials and cleans partial install state; note it is currently available in the release candidate.

Coordinate comms with documentation readiness: once the release candidate is promoted, customer communication should not point users to the old installer flow; docs must include the new recovery command, credential cleanup steps, and updated screenshots (Windows screenshots pending).

openQuestions: Has Windows recovery (including the credential-cache issue) been fully validated, and if not, what is the current ETA and interim safe guidance for Windows users?
Is the release candidate being promoted broadly now, and are the public docs updated to remove old installer-flow references before that happens?
What exact single “safe command” should be used as the universal next step for partial-install users across macOS/Linux (and potentially Windows once validated)?
What user-facing symptom checklist should be included up front to help users self-identify as partially installed vs. unaffected vs. already recovered?
recommendation: Publish a recovery briefing with a consistent, segmented structure: (1) First paragraph: who is affected + explicit environment validation status (macOS/Linux verified; Windows pending). (2) Symptoms-first section focusing on partial-install indicators. (3) One safe next action: use the patched CLI recovery command (from the release candidate) as the primary path. (4) Environment-specific notes mirroring support macros (macOS verified, Linux verified, Windows validation in progress). (5) Documentation alignment: link only to updated docs/screenshots for the patched flow and avoid pointing users to the old installer path once the RC is promoted.
